caption:
🚨 if you want to eat here, you’ll want to make a reservation🚨

Hey foodies, if you’re headed to Japan / Tokyo, this is one for your bucket list. The first thing you need to know is that you need to book because this is reservations only and it can be tricky to book because it fills up quite quickly. I could really only get a booking for 2pm on the day I landed. There were no other spots available. Hence the bag checking and rushing to Yoyogi after landing in #hanedaairport 

So check their social media for flavours, but the strawberry crème brûlée was the one I got. And this was a beautiful desert. It’s a stunner that took a bit of time to make, and had flaky delicate snow in it.

🔥🍧🇯🇵 📍@azukitokouri #azukitokouri 

#japan #japanfood #japanfoodie #japanlocal #japandesserts #azukitokouri #sydneyfoodies #sydneyfood #tokyo #tokyofoodie #japanese #travel #melbournefood #kakigori #japantrip
transcript: So you gotta excuse the butcher pronunciation for this one. This is Izuki Tokuri in Yoyogi in Tokyo. This is a very small restaurant and it's reservations only, so you'll need to book. For me, the only day I could book was the day I landed in Tokyo. So I had to check my bags in and then had to navigate my way to Yoyogi for my booking at about 2 o'clock in the afternoon. So you come here because the dessert, the Kakigori, is made by a pastry chef who used to work in a Michelin-starred restaurant in Paris. You can see from the process of making this that it's quite labored and crafted. There's an artisanal quality to this and there's a short list of flavors. This is the one I wanted for the spectacle. I didn't travel all the way to Japan to eat a boring-ass Kakigori. This is the one. The scent of burnt sugar. The snow is so light and flaky and delicious. This is kind of sensational, so it's clear to see why it's hard to get into. So book early, bookmark this and follow for more.
